metadata.title:
REFUSAL MEANS SHORT RE.
NEWAL CONTRACT AND IN
CREASED EFFORTS ON BE.
HALF OF CITY PlANT-lAT-
TER MEANS SAVING OF
THOUSANDS.

pages.raw_text:
The probability of a municipal
lighting plant in the near future
is more apparent than ever. The
5-year contract of the Washington
Water Power Co. to furnish the
city with lights expires August 81
and the council, unless the w. w.
P. gets particularly busy with a
substantial reduction in it's 81 rate,
will limit the term of the next con-
tsact to l year. In the meantime
some definite action toward the in
stallation of a city piant may be
taken.


Five years ago the board of pub
lie works gave the w. w. p. fat
contract at 81 a month for each arc
light. There was no opposition
from any other electric company
because there is no other cleo
tricity producing company in Spo-
kane.


Although extremely high, the
board let the contract. At that
time there were 249 arc lights dis
tributed about the city at various
street corners. But since then
about 100 more have been put in at
various times. The council order
ed the board to install 8 additional
at l time in each ward. In 1903, on
recommendation of l.. Frank Boyd
